source: Representation Learning with Contrastive Predictive Coding

concepts.finding: CPC: self-supervised representation learning via autoregressive prediction in latent space. Learns to predict future latent representations from past context using contrastive loss (noise-contrastive estimation). Applied to audio, images, text, and RL. Encodes mutual information between context and future observations.
concepts.implication: Keyword embedding via predictive coding: predict next keyword in a search session from current keyword embedding. Contrastive loss with negative sampling. Self-supervised, no labels needed. Captures temporal intent transitions.
concepts.open_question: Can contrastive predictive coding on search session sequences produce better keyword embeddings than simple co-occurrence (word2vec)?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: CPC-trained keyword embeddings (predicting next query in session) capture more predictive intent signals than word2vec embeddings on the same session data.
